description: baseline roadmap metric failed because stage0 cargo check cannot run from compiler/ relative manifest path

asi.hypothesis: initialize honest roadmap completion metric and validation harness
asi.error_details: ./autoresearch.sh exited before metrics; likely manifest paths need repository-root prefixes because cargo manifests reference workspace paths
asi.rollback_reason: No code implementation yet; despite forward-motion preference, autoresearch crash logging may revert harness changes automatically
asi.next_action_hint: Recreate/fix harness using compiler/stage0 and compiler/rt paths or set working directory correctly, then rerun baseline

description: baseline roadmap metric harness on main with forward-motion constraints

asi.hypothesis: a simple honest completion-count metric plus cargo check/runtime tests can guide PLAN implementation without overfitting
asi.user_constraint: Work on main; forward/additive manner; never intentionally revert iterations; always commit and move forward
asi.baseline: 0 of 50 PLAN entries are marked complete; stage0 cargo check and rt tests pass in the harness

asi.hypothesis: removing the main-block one-output cap should allow ordinary ordered effect sequences without backend changes
asi.implemented: Removed validation error for output_actions > 1 in main; converted the old two-statements diagnostic into programs/basic/sequenced_main.ml with stdout proving first/second/third order; marked PLAN step 1 done
asi.validation: sequenced fixture passed with snapshot update; full stage0 fixtures passed (140 tests); stage0 cargo check passed; rt tests passed serially
asi.next_action_hint: Proceed to step 2 structural RtValue equality. Inspect rt RtValue representation and codegen equality lowering; current string equality likely uses static/printed paths.

asi.hypothesis: boxed equality should be semantic structural equality in the runtime, while unboxed scalar equality stays direct LLVM compare
asi.implemented: Added riot_rt_value_eq with recursive equality for unit/bool/i64/pid/string/tuple/list/record; lowered non-scalar codegen equality through ABI; fixed Static-to-RtValue conversion so static compound values are not rendered strings for semantic equality; added nested rt unit test and structural_equality fixture plus mixed string/i64 diagnostic
asi.validation: stage0 cargo check passed; rt tests passed serially (6 tests); focused structural equality and equality diagnostic fixtures passed; emitted LLVM contains calls to riot_rt_value_eq for boxed comparisons
asi.next_action_hint: Proceed to step 3: add riot_rt_value_lt for boxed i64/string, extend codegen Lt fallback to runtime ABI, add positive string/i64 comparison fixtures and diagnostics for tuple/list/record ordering.

description: feat(rt): add RtValue ordering for i64 and strings

asi.hypothesis: runtime ordering for boxed strings/i64 plus checker rejection for unsupported boxed types completes the next vertical comparison slice without affecting direct scalar codegen
asi.implemented: Added riot_rt_value_lt; runtime compares boxed i64 payloads and string bytes only; codegen emits direct signed LLVM compare for i64/i64 and runtime ABI calls for boxed/static cases; checker rejects tuple/list/record and mixed/non-comparable ordering; added runtime_ordering fixture and three diagnostics
asi.validation: run_experiment passed with stage0 cargo check and serial rt checks; focused fixtures and rt ordering unit passed; emitted LLVM for runtime_ordering contains riot_rt_value_lt calls for string comparisons
asi.next_action_hint: Proceed to step 4 record field access. There are existing parser fixtures named record_field_access, likely static-only; inspect AST path/dotted parsing and replace/prevent static precomputation by adding runtime record get ABI and RIR/codegen node.

description: feat(stage0): lower record field access for runtime values

asi.hypothesis: making lowercase dotted access an explicit Field node and lowering it to a runtime record lookup will eliminate static precomputation for record projection
asi.implemented: Added AstExpr/TypedExpr/RirExpr Field; parser now treats lowercase postfix dots as fields and uppercase dotted paths as module paths; added riot_rt_value_record_get ABI and codegen lowering; field access is ABI Value; added runtime unit coverage for record get and runtime_record_field fixture
asi.validation: run_experiment passed with stage0 cargo check and serial rt checks; focused runtime_record_field fixture passed; existing record_field_access and record_nested_access fixtures passed with snapshots updated; emit ir for runtime_record_field contains Field nodes
asi.next_action_hint: Proceed to step 5 tuple projection. Reuse the Field/postfix structure for numeric suffixes like tuple.0, add runtime tuple_get ABI, and add projection fixtures for literals, bindings, function returns, nested tuples plus static OOB diagnostic where possible.

asi.hypothesis: numeric postfix projection can reuse the field-access pipeline with a dedicated tuple projection node and runtime ABI
asi.implemented: Added AstExpr/TypedExpr/RirExpr TupleIndex; parser accepts numeric dotted suffixes; added riot_rt_value_tuple_get ABI and codegen lowering; added tuple_projection fixture for literal/local/function-return/nested projections and tuple_projection_oob diagnostic for static tuple OOB
asi.validation: run_experiment passed with stage0 cargo check and serial rt checks; focused tuple_projection fixture/diagnostic passed; rt compound-value test covers tuple_get; emitted LLVM contains riot_rt_value_tuple_get calls
asi.known_limitation: Chained numeric projection without parentheses like nested().0.1 currently lexes ambiguously; fixture uses (nested().0).1. Consider lexer/postfix refinement later.
asi.next_action_hint: Proceed to step 6 list length and nth operations. Prefer prelude/source-level functions list_len/list_get lowered as ordinary known calls to runtime ABI, with runtime unit tests and fixtures for empty/non-empty length and scalar/boxed indexing.
